finalization.note: Blocked until the three Stage-1 categories exist: candidate container digests (base + per-arch builder), candidate dependency lock hashes, and candidate verifier-material manifests. Guest closure (r0_guest_set_hash, guest-program identities, populated allowlist) is post-spec-hash and is NOT required here. No fabricated values are inserted; protocol_hash refuses to run until then.

toolchains.validator_tool_rust_floor_note: Minimum Rust for building/running the B0-PRE validator tool crates.
toolchains.candidate_container_rust_note: Separately-frozen toolchain for candidate proving containers; distinct from the validator-tool floor and not conflated with it.

unit_kind_rules.rule: Single frozen layer group; runs RMSNorm+attention+FFN and emits the residual/KV state. selected_token and eos are sentinels.
Projects the final residual to logits and selects the argmax token (lowest index on ties); empty prior-KV and empty output manifest.

schema_layouts.name: DerivedInputV1
schema_layouts.note: ids, model_id, commitment identities, position, sequence_length

schema_layouts.name: R0ComputationStatementV2
schema_layouts.note: full public statement; template zeroes b0_pre_spec_hash

schema_layouts.name: GuestProgramAllowlistV1(empty)
schema_layouts.note: schema_version(2) + count(4); only the EMPTY allowlist schema + hashing rule is in the spec hash. A populated allowlist (228 header + 33 bytes/entry) is post-spec.

schema_layouts.name: R0ProofArtifactEnvelopeV1
schema_layouts.note: proof envelope with embedded artifact bytes

commitment_merkle.identity_rule: ObjectCommitmentV1 = OBJECT_TAG || kind || byte_len || chunk_count || merkle_root; identity() = BLAKE3 over the 80-byte encoding.
commitment_merkle.merkle_rule: chunk_count = ceil(byte_len / CHUNK); leaves are BLAKE3 of each chunk; binary Merkle over leaves; single leaf is its own root.
commitment_merkle.empty_object_rule: Empty object: byte_len 0, chunk_count 0, all-zero merkle_root.

strict_scan: UTF-8 only; reject BOM and control chars outside strings
reject leading zeros, '+' signs, and bare/ trailing decimal points
reject NaN, Infinity, and exponent-only anomalies
reject trailing bytes after the top-level value
integers above u64::MAX are preserved via arbitrary_precision, never coerced to f64
canonical: object keys sorted by UTF-8 byte order
minimal number form: no leading zeros, no exponent, no trailing zeros
the canonical byte stream is ASCII; any non-ASCII character is \u-escaped, and the minimal escape set is used
null is not permitted; absent optional fields are omitted, never null

exp_table.lookup_rule: z <= Z_MAX reads table[z]; z > Z_MAX yields 0.
exp_table.certification_rule: Each entry is the unique Q16 rounding of exp(-z/SCALE) proven by a big-rational interval enclosure; no floating point.

transformer.attention_rule: 2 heads, head_dim 4, masked softmax; score / 512; weights from the exp table; denominator floored at 65536
transformer.requantize_rule: RHAZ(value, 256) - round half away from zero
transformer.select_argmax_rule: argmax over logits; lowest index wins ties

official_statements.note: The only two canonical B0-PRE workload statements. Both are byte-locked, independently, by both tool crates (NON_SELECTION / TEST_ONLY weights).

contract_steps: authenticate model + inputs against the public commitments
reconstruct DerivedInputV1 and check its commitment
enforce frozen dims/bounds and the checked max_state_bytes
execute the frozen unit and recompute the output manifest
rebuild the full statement template and compare byte-for-byte

rules: Exactly two candidates (SP1=1, RISC0=2); 0 is not a candidate.

At R0 time (after b0_pre_spec_hash is finalized), a candidate is eligible only with complete, non-mixed evidence bound to BOTH hashes: b0_pre_spec_hash and the post-spec r0_guest_set_hash, plus its container digest, tool lock hashes, guest-program identities, and verifier material.
Candidate proving containers build under the candidate-container toolchain (Rust 1.88.0), distinct from the validator-tool floor (Rust 1.85.0).

OmniNode contributor eligibility is device-neutral: no minimum CPU, RAM, GPU, storage, or device class determines whether a contributor is protocol-eligible. A valid proof from any device is eligible; a slower device only takes longer. Prover time, memory, cores, architecture, GPU, storage, and timing variance are reported-only and never determine eligibility, qualification, or the B0-FINAL tie-break.

evidence_completeness.grid_rule: Measured grid: 2 statements x 2 architectures x 10 iterations = 40 measured proofs, in canonical ascending order.
evidence_completeness.non_mixability_rule: Every record must bind the same spec/guest-set/candidate/ material/program/lock/container identities; mixed evidence is rejected.

qualification_gates.validator_eligibility: Validator qualification is performance-based, not hardware-class-based: no minimum physical cores or RAM, and detected host hardware is never an eligibility gate. A node of any CPU/RAM configuration may participate; reference_cpuset_cores / reference_memory_bytes are only the controlled candidate-comparison envelope, not a deployment or consensus hardware minimum. Operators remain responsible for network liveness under their workload; a machine that cannot keep the verification pace has an operational capacity condition, not a proof-system disqualification or consensus invalidity.
qualification_gates.scope: Chain-side proof verification under a controlled reference envelope: the verification run is pinned to exactly reference_cpuset_cores cores and reference_memory_bytes of memory (2 cores / 4 GiB). Detected host hardware need only be sufficient to establish those limits and is never gated. This is a candidate-comparison envelope, not a validator hardware minimum.
gates: host_verify_ns p99 (nearest-rank, host_setup_ns excluded) must be <= verify_p99_gate_ns on the worst architecture

aggregate_verify_ns_per_block = worst_arch_p99_verify_ns * max_accepted_proofs_per_block (checked; overflow disqualifies) must be <= aggregate_verify_budget_ns_per_block; evaluated INDEPENDENTLY of the per-proof p99 gate (their product equalling the budget is a coincidence)

the verification run must be configured to exactly reference_cpuset_cores cores and reference_memory_bytes memory; detected host hardware is not an eligibility gate, but provenance must be self-consistent (configured limits <= detected resources, all nonzero)

each candidate's result_set.verifier_material_bytes must equal the Sum of byte_len over THAT candidate's own preregistered verifier-material manifest, checked independently per candidate. There is no cross-candidate verifier-material byte constant and no universal maximum: SP1's 292-byte Groth16 VK is SP1's value only, never a shared gate.
max_cycles is a reported bound; official statements set it to 0
qualification_failure_codes: 4 = aggregate (worst-arch p99 * max_accepted_proofs_per_block, checked) exceeds aggregate_verify_budget_ns_per_block, or the multiplication overflows

qualifies_on: correctness against the frozen witness-binding contract
a valid terminal proof verified through the pinned verifier
verifier identity / security (verifier material bound and authentic)
reproducibility (digest-pinned container, lock hashes, guest identities)
cross-architecture semantic equivalence (x86_64 and aarch64 agree)
validator-side verification limits (verify p99 and per-block budget)
never_disqualifies: device architecture, GPU use, or storage usage
device class or absolute host size
qualification_criteria.note: A valid candidate is never disqualified by prover performance or device resources, and none of these enter the B0-FINAL tie-break.

contributor_resource_policy.hardware_eligibility: none: no minimum CPU, RAM, GPU, storage, or device class gates OmniNode participation. Preregistration correction removing the former >=16-core / >=64-GiB / 35%-cap proving-resource gate (resource_gate_proving).

local_resource_budget.scope: A recommended default local resource-budget an OmniNode operator may configure for their device. It is not consensus, proof validity, candidate selection, or hardware eligibility.

per_architecture_controls: same detected physical and logical core counts
same configured cpuset and memory limit
same governor, turbo state, clock source, and cgroup version/scope
same workload, warmup, and iteration policy
fair_benchmark_pairing.rule: For each (architecture, provenance role) the two candidates' provenance must represent the same controlled host and environment; this is enforced in the paired-evidence verification path, not merely documented. Candidate-specific identities (guest program, lock hash, verifier material, container digest) are not compared. No particular absolute host size is required (device neutrality), so weaker hardware only takes longer -- but the two paired candidates may not run on different hardware.

contributor_resource_policy.prove_watchdog: Run-management timeout only. A timeout produces an incomplete run requiring continuation/retry; it is not a candidate performance failure or a disqualification.
contributor_resource_policy.note: Neither validators nor contributors have hardware-class eligibility. Validator qualification is performance-based under a controlled reference envelope (see qualification_gates); contributors have no resource gate at all. A valid proof remains valid regardless of the device that produced it, and a validator that cannot keep the verification pace has an operational-liveness condition, not a consensus disqualification.

deterministic_rules: Samples sorted by (arch, statement, iteration); aggregates recomputed from raw bytes.
p99 via nearest-rank; bundle hashes are domain-prefixed BLAKE3 over the sorted raw records.
b0_final_tiebreak: Among qualified candidates, break ties by lowest verify-p99, then lowest proof_bytes, then lowest candidate discriminant (SP1 before RISC0).
tiebreak_excludes: core count, device architecture, GPU, or storage

hash_preimage_rules.canonical_json_rule: b0_pre_spec_hash = BLAKE3(SPEC_PREFIX || canonical_protocol_json), where the canonical JSON follows the canonical rules above.
hash_preimage_rules.finalization_rule: The preimage may only be built from a finalized artifact; a not_finalizable artifact (any Stage-1 input absent) cannot be hashed. r0_guest_set_hash and guest-program identities are post-spec and never enter this preimage.

stage1_spec_hash_includes: the complete normative protocol and workload
immutable base and per-architecture builder container identities
candidate verifier-material manifests and their identities
both official zero-hash statement templates and their template hashes
statement materialization rule: insert b0_pre_spec_hash into the template's b0_pre_spec_hash range [34,66) to form each final statement
the empty GuestProgramAllowlistV1 schema and its hashing rules
provenance/result-set schemas, eligibility rules, aggregation rules, failure codes
post_spec_hash_steps: 1. materialize the two final statements by inserting b0_pre_spec_hash
3. derive guest image / vkey / program identities
6. bind all R0 evidence to both b0_pre_spec_hash and r0_guest_set_hash
lifecycle.note: Two stages, in order. Stage 1 hashes only the material above into b0_pre_spec_hash before any guest exists. Guests then embed that hash, so r0_guest_set_hash and guest identities are derived afterwards and can never feed back into the spec-hash preimage.
